prevalence.trend: increasing in urban settings with diet change and sedentary behavior

burden.dalyPerCapita: low; high QOL and caregiver burden

vulnerablePopulations: children with limited access to pediatric care
epidemiology.diagnosticCriteria: Rome IV functional constipation criteria; evaluate red flags for organic causes

publicHealthSystem.controlRate: Variable; adherence and follow-up constraints are common

regionalSpecificities.diagnosisCriteria: Often diagnosed clinically; limited access to pediatric GI outside cities
regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Education, toilet routine, dietary fiber/fluids, osmotic laxatives; address behavioral components
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Low follow-up continuity and variable access to PEG and dietary counseling
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: Most cases manageable in primary care with education and inexpensive therapy
